layman_headline: Fear is high and altcoins look weak — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is sitting in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 25/100, with Bitcoin dominating at 56.5% and sucking capital away from smaller coins. While a few altcoins like Lido DAO have bounced sharply (+27% in a week), they've already moved too far too fast to safely enter now. The candidates that are genuinely oversold (BONK, NIGHT) have terrible chart structures with strong bearish patterns, making them falling knives rather than buying opportunities.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The best setups will come when these oversold coins show actual bottoming signals — like a reversal candle at support — rather than just being 'down a lot.' Set price alerts on LDO around $0.35 and ETH around $1,800 for potential entries.
expert_reasoning: Surveying all 8 swing candidates reveals a market in distress with no clean entry. BONK has a strongly bearish chart (-2.0 bias) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — classic capitulation candles that warn of further downside despite RSI near 31. NIGHT is down 40% in a week with no support levels identified and bearish chart bias. LDO is the strongest technically with a +0.85 chart bias and Three White Soldiers, but it's already extended (+51.7% in 5 days) with a terrible R/R of 0.38x to nearest resistance. The BinanceLife token shows a Bull Flag but has RSI at 58 and poor R/R (0.97x). With Fear & Greed at 25 and BTC dominance at 56.5%, capital is rotating into BTC and away from alts — fighting this trend is inadvisable. The prudent move is to wait for LDO to pull back toward its $0.30 support or for oversold coins to form genuine reversal patterns.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to crush altcoin performance — any further BTC strength drains alt liquidity
Extreme Fear (25) can persist or deepen before a true reversal — catching knives in this environment has poor odds
No funding rate data available for any candidate, leaving leveraged positioning blind spots
watch_list: LDO price action around $0.35-0.37 support zone — a pullback with a bullish reversal candle would be a textbook entry
Fear & Greed index — a move from 25 toward 35+ would signal improving risk appetite for alt entries
BTC holding $65,000 support — if BTC breaks down, all alt setups become invalid
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC itself is showing modest recovery (+4.5% weekly) but altcoins are heavily mixed, with many still in steep downtrends.
context_banner: Extreme Fear and high BTC dominance make this a patience-over-action environment — the best trade is no trade until setups improve.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 25 (Extreme Fear) with flat trend at 26.3 weekly average — sentiment hasn't bottomed convincingly, making contrarian entries premature.
BONK shows Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu (chart bias -2.0), confirming active selling pressure despite approaching oversold RSI — a falling knife, not a bounce candidate.
LDO RSI at 59 after a +51.7% 5-day rally means the strongest chart setup is already extended; entering now offers only 0.38x risk/reward to nearest resistance.
Grayscale filing S-1 for a Worldcoin ETF and CLARITY bill developments show institutional crypto adoption continues, but no immediate catalyst for the specific altcoins on the watchlist.
ETH derivatives reset narrative and DePIN/gaming rebound suggest sector rotation is underway but hasn't produced a clean swing setup in current candidates.
